mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in the industrial production index over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in aggregate demand that drives S&P 500 forward returns positively.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average return in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the housing starts index over the next 60 trading days signals a supply-side shock that drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average return in at least 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 50-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate over the next 20 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average return in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5-percentage-point increase in the unemployment rate over the next 5 trading days signals a labor market slowdown that drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average return in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in the real personal income index and a 10% increase in the housing starts index over the next 20 trading days signal a surge in aggregate demand that drives S&P 500 forward returns positively.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the 20-day moving average return in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in the industrial production index and a 5% increase in the housing starts index over the next 60 trading days signal a supply-side shock that drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 10% in 3 months and HOUST rises > 5% in 3 months
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average return in at least 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 25-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 5-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average return in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.25-percentage-point increase in the unemployment rate over the next 60 trading days signals a labor market slowdown that drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the 60-day forward return is not significantly lower than the 20-day moving average return in at least 70% of instances.
